landing.title: Less chaos. More teaching.
landing.description: TeacherHelper helps you organize examples cleanly, build tests faster and manage teaching content together in everyday school life — modern, clear and without unnecessary clutter.

points.p1: Build tests from examples in just a few clicks
points.p2: Reuse content over time instead of doing everything twice
points.p3: Manage everything together with colleagues in one school space

trust.t1: Faster workflow instead of material chaos
trust.t3: Built with print, PDF and Word in mind

visual.poolTitle: Your example pool at a glance
visual.poolText: Find tasks faster, combine content in a smart way and create clean tests with only a few clicks.

mock.title1: Calculate the missing values in the ratio task

features.title: Built for real lesson preparation
features.subtitle: Not overloaded, but practical. TeacherHelper helps exactly where planning, collecting material and creating tests usually become frustrating.

f1.title: Organize examples centrally
f1.text: Store tasks in one place instead of losing them across folders, chats and old files.

f2.title: Filter and find quickly
f2.text: Find content by type, topic or focus instantly when you need it for the next test.

f3.title: Create tests much faster
f3.text: Build complete tests from existing examples in just a few steps instead of starting from zero every time.

f4.title: Clean preview for export and print
f4.text: Check everything before printing and keep a clear output for PDF, Word and print.

f5.title: Collaboration inside your school team
f5.text: Work with colleagues in a shared school environment and keep content where it is actually needed.

f6.title: Made for real schools
f6.text: No unnecessary feature clutter, just a workflow that actually fits teachers, departments and daily school life.

pricing.title: A plan for every stage
pricing.subtitle: Start for free, work more professionally with Pro or use TeacherHelper as a shared platform for your school.

free.subtitle: Perfect for trying it out and small setups.

pro.subtitle: For active teachers who regularly prepare material and tests.

school.subtitle: For schools, departments and shared management across the whole staff.

final.title: Turn material chaos into one clean workflow.
final.text: Start for free and see how much faster lesson preparation can become with a clear interface.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How TeacherHelper collects, uses, and stores data.

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: TeacherHelper processes personal and usage-related data only to provide the platform and its core features.
We aim to collect as little data as possible and do not sell personal data.

sections.heading: What data may be collected
bullets: Account data such as username and email address
School, test, and example content created inside the app
Authentication-related technical data such as login or session information
Uploaded images or other files you explicitly provide

sections.heading: Why data is used
bullets: To provide login and account management
To save and display school, test, and example data
To keep the application secure and stable
To process support or deletion requests

sections.heading: Storage and third-party services
paragraphs: Data may be stored in databases, hosting systems, and cloud storage used to operate the application.
Third-party infrastructure providers are only used to run the service and not for advertising purposes.

sections.heading: Your rights
bullets: Access to your stored data
Correction of incorrect data
Deletion of your account or stored content where applicable
Questions about how your data is processed

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Rules and conditions for using TeacherHelper.

sections.heading: Use of the platform
paragraphs: TeacherHelper is provided to help organize educational content such as examples and tests.
By using the platform, you agree to use it lawfully and responsibly.

sections.heading: Allowed use
bullets: Use the application only for legitimate educational or organizational purposes
Keep your account credentials secure
Respect the rights and privacy of other users

sections.heading: Not allowed
bullets: Uploading unlawful, harmful, or abusive content
Attempting to disrupt, reverse engineer, or misuse the service
Using the platform in a way that could damage data integrity or availability

sections.heading: Availability and warranty
paragraphs: The application is provided on an “as is” basis without any guarantee of uninterrupted availability.
Features may change, be improved, or be removed at any time.

sections.heading: Limitation of liability
paragraphs: The operator is not liable for indirect damages, data loss, or interruptions unless required by applicable law.

sections.heading: Project
paragraphs: TeacherHelper

sections.heading: Operator
paragraphs: Michael Leisch

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: michael.leisch@gmx.at

sections.heading: Responsibility for content
paragraphs: The operator is responsible for the content of this application in accordance with applicable law.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: Information about cookies and similar technologies.

sections.heading: Essential cookies
paragraphs: TeacherHelper may use essential cookies or similar local storage mechanisms required for login, authentication, security, and user preferences.

sections.heading: No marketing cookies
paragraphs: TeacherHelper does not intentionally use advertising or marketing cookies.

sections.heading: Managing cookies
paragraphs: You can usually remove cookies or local storage entries through your browser settings. Note that doing so may log you out or reset saved preferences.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Need help, found a bug, or want to suggest a feature?

sections.heading: GitHub Issues (recommended)
paragraphs: The preferred support channel for this project is GitHub Issues.
Please use it for bug reports, feature requests, or reproducible technical problems.

sections.heading: Personal support
paragraphs: If your request is private, account-related or not suitable for GitHub, you can contact us directly.
Email: michael.leisch@gmx.at

sections.heading: Before creating an issue
bullets: Check whether the problem already exists
Describe what happened and what you expected
Add screenshots if useful
Include steps to reproduce the issue

sections.heading: Response time
paragraphs: Support requests are handled as quickly as possible, but response times may vary depending on availability.

help.title: Help Center
help.subtitle: A quick overview, clear orientation, and a changelog that shows what actually got better.

docs.headline: The important parts at a glance
docs.subline: No giant manual — just what actually matters in daily use.

collections.title: Collection system
collections.text: Collections give you one clear place for tasks, tests, and shared content, so everything stays organized instead of being scattered around.

examplesTests.title: Examples & tests
examplesTests.text: Create strong examples once, reuse them later, and turn them into new tests quickly — that is exactly what TeacherHelper is for.

searchFilter.title: Search & filters
searchFilter.text: As your content grows, finding things well becomes essential. Search and filters help you get back to exactly what you need much faster.

invites.title: Cooperation & teamwork
invites.text: Invite members and manage your tasks and tests together in one shared collection.

changelog.headline: What has improved
changelog.subline: Short, clear, and without the marketing fluff.
changelog.label: Release

v100.title: The foundation is there
v100.description: With 1.0.0, the base was in place: organizing content, managing users, and building the first clean test workflows.
v100.c1: Created the foundation for the collection system
v100.c2: Integrated a user system with accounts and management
v100.c3: Launched examples and tests as the main core features

v110.title: More structure in daily use
v110.description: 1.1.0 improves TeacherHelper especially where clarity and speed matter most in everyday work.
v110.c1: Added a folder system so content can be structured more cleanly
v110.c2: Use variables to make examples more flexible and reusable
v110.c3: Added filters and search to find examples and tests much faster

h1.title: Get oriented fast
h1.text: The help page gives you the most important areas at a glance, without forcing you through endless blocks of text.

h2.title: Updates are easy to spot
h2.text: The changelog lets you see what was added and what got better for everyday use, right away.

h3.title: Tutorial area is already prepared
h3.text: The guided walkthrough is coming next. The place for it already exists so users can restart it any time later.

sidecard.title: One starting point for help, updates, and later also guides
sidecard.text: This page should feel less like dry documentation and more like a clean hub for everything that gives orientation inside TeacherHelper.
sidecard.p1: Short explanations instead of overloaded manuals
sidecard.p2: Clear release notes without unnecessary fluff
sidecard.p3: Room for interactive tours and guided tutorials

tutorialCard.title: Guided tutorial is coming next
tutorialCard.text: This is already the entry point for the future app walkthrough. Later, it can guide users page by page and explain what they can do there.
